title: • Today's Intention: My goal today is not to speak, but to listen—deepl
content:
• Today's Intention: My goal today is not to speak, but to listen—deeply and without planning my response.
    • NIGHT
    • Night Reading: "‘You have learned that from the river too,’ said Siddhartha, ‘that one must go down to the depths.’"
    • Reflection: What did I learn by truly listening today? What did the river of my own day—the flow of events and conversations—teach me?
    Day 20
    MORNING
    • Morning Reading: "But the river’s voice was not only one voice, it was many voices... all these voices, all these goals, all these desires, all this suffering, all this pleasure, all this good and evil, all of them together was the world."
    • Today's Intention: I will accept all my emotions today—the happy and the sad—as valid parts of my own "river."
    NIGHT
    • Night Reading: "He learned to listen. He learned to listen with a still heart, with a waiting, open soul, without passion, without desire, without judgment, without opinion."
    • Reflection: What conflicting "voices" did I hear within myself today? Can I hold them all without judgment?
context: From document: A 30-Day Journey with Siddhartha: Aligning the Path with Daily Life — chunk 21 (chunk 21/29)
